LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A., AND, CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO RELIEF BASED ON CIVIL CODE 1632 TO GIVE PLAINTIFFS AN OPPORTUNITY, IF THEY ARE ABLE TO ALLEGE WHO WERE THE REAL ESTATE BROKERS AND HOW THE ACCOUNTS' COMPLEXITY REQUIRE AN ACCOUNTING ABSENT A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE RELIEF BASED ON CIVIL CODE 2923.5. MS. HARRIS TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER FOR THE COURT'S SIGNATURE. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, REPORTER: BRENDA FIELDS, CSR #7235
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED SUSTAINING WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN PART AND OVERRULING IN PART FILED BY DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY

DECLARATION OF KATHERINE S AGBAYANI REPONSE TO OSC RE: FAILURE TO FILE AND SERVE NTC OF ORDER REGARDING SUSTAINING OF DEMURRER FILED BY DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A., CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION (DECLARATORY RELIEF) AS WITHDRAWN. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION (B&P SEC. 17200). PLAINTIFF STATES A CLAIM FOR UNLAWFUL BUSINESS PRACTICE BASED ON DEFENDANTS' VIOLATIONS OF CC SEC. 2923.5. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 3RD (CC SEC. 1572) AND 4TH (INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION.) CAUSES OF ACTION TO PLEAD FRAUD WITH MORE PARTICULARITY. DEMURER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION (IIED). PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PLEAD OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-27-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A., CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Christine B. Yeo, Esq. of Law Office of Gene W. Choe (213) 639-3888 Appearing thru CourtCall for Plaintiffs; Macey Chan, Esq. of Adorno, Yoss, Alvarado & Smith (714) 852-6800 Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendants and Moving party. (This motion is not being contested.) The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled as to 1st cause of action (declaratory relief) as withdrawn. Demurrer overruled as to 2nd cause of action (B&P sec. 17200). Plaintiff states a claim for unlawful business practice based on defendants' violations of CC sec. 2923.5. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 3rd (CC sec. 1572) and 4th (Intentional misrepresentation.) Causes of action to plead fraud with more particularity. Demurer sustained without leave to amend as to 5th cause of action (IIED). Plaintiff fails to plead outrageous conduct. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A., AND, CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Gary Poteet, Esq. Law Offices of Gene W. Choe, (213) 639-3888 Appearing thru CourtCall, for Plaintiffs and opposing party; LaShan Harris, Esq. of Adorno, Yoss Alvarado & Smith (714) 852-6800 Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to relief based on Civil Code 1632 to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to allege who were the real estate brokers and how the accounts' complexity require an accounting absent a fiduciary relationship. The demurrer is overruled as to the relief based on Civil Code 2923.5. Ms. Harris to prepare proposed Order for the Court's signature. (302/HEK) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, REPORTER: BRENDA FIELDS, CSR # 7235
